S70.result: 3-line induction: strict grammar → all values real → i unreachable

S71.title: Extended closure computed
S71.result: N=1..7: all real until N=4 (negative reals), N=5 first complex Im=-π

S72.title: iπ algebra traced
S72.result: exp(iπ)=-1, Log(iπ)=ln(π)+iπ/2. Loop: -1↔iπ. Im=1 requires arg=-1.

S73.title: Closure proof attempt
S73.result: Tan(1) obstruction: Im=1 requires arg(y)=-1 for constructible y. tan(1) transcendental.

S74.title: Lean T19
S74.result: StrictBarrier.lean: type-level proof via StrictTree.eval : ... → Option ℝ

S75.title: Lean extended framework
S75.result: ExtendedClosure.lean: loophole proved, ipi-euler proved, 3 sorries for transcendence

S76.title: Catalog
S76.result: T19 (THEOREM) + T_i (CONJECTURE) written up with full history

conjectures_strengthened.name: Extended-Grammar i-Conjecture
conjectures_strengthened.new_evidence: tan(1) obstruction identified; structural propagation rule formalized

key_mathematical_insight:
The strict vs extended grammar distinction is SHARP:
  Strict: trivial (type-level). All values are ℝ.
  Extended: subtle (transcendence theory required).
  The 'loophole' is the ln of a negative real, introducing Im = -π.
  But -π cannot produce 1 via finite EML composition without a
  constructible value whose argument is exactly 1 radian,
  which requires tan(1) ∈ EML-reachable — not known to be true.
open_questions: Is tan(1) in EML({1}, extended)? (Seems no, but unproved)
What is the full algebraic structure of Im(EML({1}, extended))?
Is the conjecture provable via Nesterenko's theorem (2001)?
N=10..12 search (Rust) — already running from S61
